Simon Yan

Data Scientist, Quantitative Analyst & Full-Stack Engineer

I’m a data scientist and quantitative analyst with 2 periods of hands-on experience in quantitative finance and data systems. My work bridges rigorous statistical analysis with scalable engineering—from designing multi-factor trading models to building production-grade data pipelines.

I hold a Master’s degree in Information Science (Data Science concentration) from Cornell University, with expertise in machine learning, statistical modeling, and financial data analysis. My background spans quantitative investment research at Central China Securities and full-stack development of consumer applications.

Beyond the terminal, I’m passionate about film photography, playing bass guitar, and exploring the intersection of data science and market microstructure. I’m currently exploring opportunities in data science, quantitative finance and fintech where I can drive real business impact.

Career

Professional Experience

2024/6 — 2024/8

Quantitative Investment Research Intern

Central China Securities · Shanghai, China

Optimized 10+ multi-factor alpha signals using market regime classification to enhance stability. Engineered a modular Python backtesting framework for 20+ equity strategies and architected an automated ETL pipeline processing 100K+ daily entries to support data-driven asset allocation decisions.

2020 — 2022

Financial Product Researcher Intern

Central China Securities · Zhengzhou, China

Spearheaded sector research for Semiconductor and Autonomous Driving industries, producing investment memos that guided product roadmaps. Evaluated fund performance through risk metric benchmarking (Alpha, Beta, Std Dev), resulting in the selection of 2 target funds for the firm’s FOF portfolio.:::

Education

Academic Background

2025 — Present

M.P.S Information Science

Cornell University · Ithaca, New York

Concentration: Data Science. Focuses on computational approaches to real-world problems in quantitative finance, with emphasis on factor modeling and stochastic simulation. Key coursework includes Machine Learning, Monte Carlo Simulation, and Markov Chains & Stochastic Processes.

2021 - 2025

B.A. Geography: Data Science

University of Washington · Seattle, Washington

Cumulative GPA: 3.55/4.0 | Major GPA: 3.89/4.0. Recipient of the Annual Dean’s List honors (2023-2025). Built a strong mathematical and technical foundation through coursework in Advanced Linear Algebra, Real Analysis, Data Structures & Algorithms, and Machine Learning. Specialized in applying quantitative methods to geospatial and statistical analysis.

Credentials

Certifications & Awards

Coursera Certificate

Professional Certificates in Python & SQL· 2022

Earned specialized certifications in Python for Data Science, AI & Development and Introduction to Structured Query Language (SQL), focusing on technical implementation and database management for data-driven applications.

BCG Data Science Job Simulation

BCG · 2024

Completed a simulation of the full data science lifecycle, encompassing hypothesis framing, exploratory data analysis, feature engineering, and the translation of technical modeling results into actionable business recommendations.

Beyond Work

I believe great data science is driven by curiosity. Outside of work, I’m an avid reader of science fiction, a weekend trail runner, and a contributor to open-source ML tools. I also volunteer as a mentor for aspiring data scientists through community organizations.
